children.heading: .—Annual Aircraft Procurement Plan and Certification
children.label: a .—Annual Aircraft Procurement Plan and Certification

children.text: A plan for the procurement of the aircraft specified in subsection (b) for each of the Department of the Army, the Department of the Navy, and the Department of the Air Force developed in accordance with this section.

A certification by the Secretary that both the budget for such fiscal year and the future-years defense program submitted to Congress in relation to such budget under provide for funding of the procurement of aircraft at a level that is sufficient for the procurement of the aircraft provided for in the plan under paragraph (1) on the schedule provided in the plan.section 221 of this title
Fighter aircraft.
Attack aircraft.
Bomber aircraft.
Intertheater lift aircraft.
Intratheater lift aircraft.
Intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance aircraft.
Tanker aircraft.
Remotely piloted aircraft.
Rotary-wing aircraft.
Operational support and executive lift aircraft.
Any other major support aircraft designated by the Secretary of Defense for purposes of this section.

The annual aircraft procurement plan developed for a fiscal year for purposes of subsection (a) should be designed so that the aviation force provided for under the plan is capable of supporting the national military strategy of the United States as set forth in the most recent National Defense Strategy submitted under and the most recent National Military Strategy submitted under .section 113(g) of this titlesection 153(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: A detailed program for the procurement of the aircraft specified in subsection (b) for each of the Department of the Army, the Department of the Navy, and the Department of the Air Force over the next 15 fiscal years.

children.label: B
children.text: A description of the aviation force structure necessary to meet the requirements of the national military strategy of the United States.

children.label: C
children.text: The estimated levels of annual investment funding necessary to carry out each aircraft program, together with a discussion of the procurement strategies on which such estimated levels of annual investment funding are based, set forth in aggregate for the Department of Defense and in aggregate for each military department.

children.label: D
children.text: The estimated level of annual funding necessary to operate, maintain, sustain, and support each aircraft program throughout the life-cycle of the program, set forth in aggregate for the Department of Defense and in aggregate for each military department.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of whether the cost estimate is derived from the cost estimate position of the military department concerned or from the cost estimate position of the Office of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the cost estimate position of the military department and the cost estimate position of the Office of Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation differ by more than 5 percent for any aircraft program, an annotated cost estimate difference and sufficient rationale to explain the difference;

children.label: iii
children.text: the confidence or certainty level associated with the cost estimate for each aircraft program; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a certification that the calculations from which the cost estimate is derived are based on common cost categories used by the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment for calculating the life-cycle cost of an aircraft program.

children.label: F
children.text: An assessment by the Secretary of Defense of the extent to which the combined aircraft forces of the Department of the Army, the Department of the Navy, and the Department of the Air Force meet the national security requirements of the United States.

For any cost estimate required by subparagraph (C) or (D) of paragraph (2) for any aircraft program for which the Secretary is required to include in a report under , the source of the cost information used to prepare the annual aircraft plan shall be derived from the Selected Acquisition Report data that the Secretary plans to submit to the congressional defense committees in accordance with subsection (f) of that section for the year for which the annual aircraft procurement plan is prepared.section 4351 of this title

Each annual aircraft procurement plan shall be submitted in unclassified form, and shall contain a classified annex. A summary version of the unclassified report shall be made available to the public.

children.heading: .—Assessment When Aircraft Procurement Budget Is Insufficient to Meet Applicable Requirements
children.label: d .—Assessment When Aircraft Procurement Budget Is Insufficient to Meet Applicable Requirements
children.text: If the budget for any fiscal year provides for funding of the procurement of aircraft for the Department of the Army, the Department of the Navy, or the Department of the Air Force at a level that is not sufficient to sustain the aviation force structure specified in the aircraft procurement plan for such Department for that fiscal year under subsection (a), the Secretary shall include with the defense budget materials for that fiscal year an assessment that describes the funding shortfall and discusses the risks associated with the reduced force structure of aircraft that will result from funding aircraft procurement at such level. The assessment shall be coordinated in advance with the commanders of the combatant commands.
As part of the annual plan and certification required to be submitted under this section, the Secretary shall include a report on the aircraft in the inventory of the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: The total number of aircraft in the inventory.

children.label: i
children.text: Primary aircraft.

children.label: ii
children.text: Backup aircraft.

children.label: iii
children.text: Attrition and reconstitution reserve aircraft.

children.label: i
children.text: Bailment aircraft.

children.label: ii
children.text: Drone aircraft.

children.label: iii
children.text: Aircraft for sale or other transfer to foreign governments.

children.label: iv
children.text: Leased or loaned aircraft.

children.label: v
children.text: Aircraft for maintenance training.

children.label: vi
children.text: Aircraft for reclamation.

children.label: vii
children.text: Aircraft in storage.

children.label: D
children.text: The aircraft inventory requirements approved by the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

Each report under paragraph (1) shall set forth each item specified in paragraph (2) separately for the regular component of each armed force and for each reserve component of each armed force and, for each such component, shall set forth each type, model, and series of aircraft provided for in the future-years defense program that covers the fiscal year for which the budget accompanying the plan, certification and report is submitted.

children.heading: .—Budget Defined
children.label: f .—Budget Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “budget” means the budget of the President for a fiscal year as submitted to Congress pursuant to .section 1105 of title 31
